mnemonics: Please Excuse My Dear Aunt Sally (for Logic): P - Parentheses/Order, E - Equivalences, M - Morgan's, D - Distributive, A - Absorption, S - Simplification/Identity
keyPoints: Logical equivalences are statements that are always true under the same truth conditions, unlike implications which are conditional.
De Morgan's Laws: ¬(P ∧ Q) ≡ ¬P ∨ ¬Q and ¬(P ∨ Q) ≡ ¬P ∧ ¬Q are fundamental for negating conjunctions and disjunctions.
Absorption Laws: P ∨ (P ∧ Q) ≡ P and P ∧ (P ∨ Q) ≡ P simplify expressions by 'absorbing' redundant terms.
commonMistakes: Confusing logical equivalence (≡) with a biconditional (↔). Equivalence is a meta-statement about two propositions, while a biconditional is a connective within a single proposition.
Misapplying De Morgan's Laws by forgetting to flip AND (∧) to OR (∨) and vice-versa when negating an entire compound statement.
Assuming commutativity or associativity applies to all connectives. Implication (→) is not commutative; P → Q is not equivalent to Q → P.
